items.source: Reading insights_history (relay-reachable via BL-20260529-004) — the M13 vwap strategy insight's prose contradicts its own structured row_counts.
items.title: INVESTIGATE: M13 template-analyst vwap strategy insight self-contradicts — summary_md says 'Trades 89/0 closed, WR n/a (0W/0L)' while the same row's row_counts says trades_closed=10, wins=1, losses=9
items.description: insights_history row id=1469 (endpoint=strategy, strategy_name=vwap, model_id=template:v1, generated_at 2026-05-29T20:35:17Z): summary_md renders 'Trades (total / closed): 89 / 0 ... Win rate: n/a (0W / 0L)', but the SAME row's row_counts_json is {trades:89, trades_closed:10, wins:1, losses:9, order_packages:20} and net PnL is rendered -$2.98 (which is non-zero, implying closed trades exist). So the markdown 'closed=0 / 0W-0L' disagrees with the structured 'closed=10 / 1W-9L / -$2.98'. The goal is NOT just to confirm the discrepancy but to ROOT-CAUSE it in the template analyst (src/runtime/insights/template_analyst.py + generator.py): likely two different closed-trade definitions feeding the same card — e.g. the prose 'closed' counts rows with a terminal close timestamp while row_counts 'trades_closed' counts a different status, OR a shadow-vs-live filter applied to one path but not the other (vwap is execution:shadow, 89 trades / mostly unfilled), OR a window/symbol-join mismatch. Whichever it is, the two numbers should be reconciled so the operator-facing prose matches the structured counts. Cosmetic-but-misleading: a reader sees 'vwap: 0 closed, WR n/a' in the narrative while the data says 1W/9L -$2.98.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or any session touching src/runtime/insights/. Re-pull insights_history (endpoint=strategy, vwap) and diff summary_md's closed/WR against row_counts to confirm it still skews; then trace the two closed-trade computations in template_analyst.py.
items.resolution_criteria: Identified the exact reason the template's prose 'closed/WR' diverges from its own row_counts (the two code paths named), then reconciled so a strategy insight's summary_md closed-count + win/loss match its row_counts (verify on vwap, which exposes it because it's shadow with many unfilled rows). If the divergence is intentional (e.g. prose deliberately counts only live-filled closes), document that so it's not read as a bug.
items.resolution_note: Root-caused + FIXED. recent_data() (src/runtime/insights/data_sources.py) filters WHERE t.status='closed' but does NOT select the status column, so recent_template's `[t for t in rows if t.get('status')=='closed']` matched NOTHING (key absent) and zeroed the header to 'Net $0.00 — 0W/0L' over a table of real closed trades. Fixed in src/runtime/insights/template_analyst.py::recent_template — treat the provided rows as the already-closed set (`t.get('status','closed')`), only narrowing if a status field is actually present. Extended test_template_recent_endpoint_table_rendering to assert header==table (1W/0L/$7.00). Pure code fix (Tier-1); live on the M13 card after the next deploy restarts ict-insights-generator.

items.source: Holistic exit-execution audit triggered by trend_donchian's 0%-win record (issues #2542-#2547).
items.title: High order_package ORPHAN rates across strategies — positions go DB-open / exchange-flat without a clean fill record
items.description: While auditing whether strategies exit properly, found high orphan rates in order_packages: trend_donchian 22/38 (58%), vwap 917/1460 (63%), squeeze 24/83 (29%), ict_scalp 4/13 (31%). 'Orphaned' = the reconciler found the DB-open package flat on the exchange but could not record a clean fill/PnL (vs reconciler_filled where it could). This loses PnL attribution and inflates the 'rejected'/'orphaned' noise in the journal, which can skew per-strategy performance stats and therefore the research/demotion evidence. Likely contributors: (a) the re-entry storm (fixed separately by the bar-close debounce) creating packages that never stabilise into real positions; (b) entry orders that fill-then-immediately-exchange-SL vs orders that never fill (the 341 exchange_rejected + intent_noop rejections suggest a mix); (c) the reconciler/orphan-handler not querying Bybit get_closed_pnl to attribute the realised PnL on a derivatives SL/TP fire (see order_monitor._classify_orphan_close — derivatives orphans are left 'unknown'). NOTE the exit machinery itself works (fade + ict_scalp win normally through reconciler_filled; the Chandelier trail ratchets) — this is a reconciliation/attribution + order-stabilisation issue, not a broken-exit issue.
items.trigger_condition: After the bar-debounce deploys (removes the re-entry-storm contributor), re-measure orphan rates; then root-cause the residual orphans with a live order-placement + Bybit get_closed_pnl trace for one fresh derivatives dispatch.
items.resolution_criteria: Orphan rate materially reduced and/or derivatives orphans get their realised PnL attributed (reconciler_filled with pnl) instead of orphaned-with-null-pnl. Per-strategy stats stop being polluted by phantom/orphan rows.
items.triage_note: 2026-06-01 /health-review: re-measured post bar-debounce (the audit tail now carries bar_debounce_blocked events, so the re-entry-storm contributor is deployed). order_packages pull (issue #2593, limit=40, 20 salvaged before the 55KB truncation): 18/20 recent packages still ORPHANED — but ALL are SHADOW strategies (16 vwap + 2 htf_pullback_trend_2h), i.e. data-only logging clutter, NOT a real-money position stranded. SL+TP present on all 20. The LIVE-path orphan concern (a real position going DB-open/exchange-flat with null pnl) was NOT exercised this quiet window — turtle_soup/trend_donchian(LONG-ONLY)/ict_scalp_5m all eval'd side=none, no live dispatch. So the residual orphans are dominated by shadow vwap (consistent with the 917/1460≈63% vwap rate in the original audit); the derivatives-PnL-attribution root-cause (order_monitor._classify_orphan_close) remains the open Tier-2 dev item. Graded health-review 'orders: watch'. | 2026-06-04 /health-review: SAME PATTERN. order_packages pull (snapshot?limit=15, issue #2726): 13/15 recent packages ORPHANED, ALL vwap (shadow, BTCUSDT, ltid=null) — data-only clutter. The 2 non-orphan rows are the live path working cleanly: a fresh ict_scalp_5m SHORT package (05:06:12Z) linked_trade_id=2428 status=open, and 1 closed vwap. SL+TP present on all 15. This review DID exercise the live path (ict_scalp_5m dispatched real-money trade 2428 on bybit_2, size 0.001 is_demo=0, correctly journaled + linked — no orphan). So the live-path orphan concern is still unexercised by a STRANDED real position; residual orphans remain shadow-vwap. Graded 'orders: watch'. Tier-2 derivatives-PnL-attribution dev item still open. | 2026-06-08 /health-review: NEW DATA + ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS — VERIFIED PARTIAL. The 2026-06-08 trades pull (/api/diag/journal?table=trades, issue #2974) shows the FAILURE MODE EXERCISED at scale: 8 closed trades in the 2026-06-06->2026-06-08 window, all status='closed' + exit_reason='reconciler_filled', but 6 of 8 carry **pnl=NULL**. CODE-PATH ROOT-CAUSE: src/runtime/order_monitor.py::_close_trade_from_order_status lines 3009-3175. The function takes one of TWO branches based on whether account_closed_pnl_for_trade returns a record: (a) the SUCCESS branch (lines 3075-3130) writes pnl + exit_price + pnl_percent; (b) the FALLBACK branch (lines 3131-3159) writes ONLY status='closed' + exit_reason='reconciler_filled' + notes — NO pnl. So pnl=NULL means account_closed_pnl_for_trade returned None (filter chain in src/units/accounts/clients.py::find_closed_pnl_record requires Bybit closed-pnl record's side + qty±5% + avgEntryPrice±10bps + createdTime>=opened_at_ms-2s to ALL match). VERIFIED account_id pattern (#2974 re-extracted): 5/5 bybit_1 (DEMO is_demo=1) closed htf_pullback trades carry pnl=NULL (#2482 sz=0.059 short, #2484 sz=0.04 short, #2490 sz=0.064 short, #2492 sz=0.033 short, #2494 sz=0.008 short). On bybit_2 (LIVE is_demo=0): 2 of 3 attributed cleanly (#2493 short sz=0.001 pnl=-1.70; #2489 ict_scalp short sz=0.003 pnl=+2.10) but #2491 (htf LONG sz=0.003, fired 00:51:39Z) carries pnl=NULL — the ONLY LONG in the closed set. Partial confirmation: demo dominates the NULL set (5/5 demo NULL vs 1/3 live NULL) but the bybit_2 NULL outlier suggests a SECOND failure mode. The bybit_2 NULL is a LONG with same size 0.003 as the preceding ict_scalp short #2489 — strong candidate for an intent-reduce/multiplex leg with a flipped journal direction (matches BL-20260531-001 pattern, fixed only on the notification side by PR #2476). REFINED Tier-2 FIX (two-pronged): (1) demo-fallback wider lookup — when the strict filter chain returns None on bybit demo (`account['demo']==true`), fall back to (side + closed-at-window) only; (2) for intent-reduce/close legs, the closed-pnl lookup should match by absolute position movement, or skip the direction filter when the leg is flagged reduce_only/intent_noop in notes. NOT shipped this session — Tier-2 touch on order_monitor.py live path; warrants a dedicated session. NEEDS one more diag pull (order_packages for the pkg linked to #2491) to confirm the second prong before shipping a code fix. Carry forward. | 2026-06-08 FIX SHIPPED + DEPLOYED (PR #2985, squash-merged main 1cb6b00): two-pronged code fix landed in src/units/accounts/clients.py (account_closed_pnl_for_trade: demo accounts opt into a wide (side+window) closed-pnl fallback via _bybit_closed_pnl_lookup allow_wide_fallback when the strict #1411/#1419 chain finds nothing; new reduce_leg param skips the flipped side + wrong entry filters) and src/runtime/order_monitor.py (_close_trade_from_order_status detects intent_reduce legs via setup_type/notes and forwards reduce_leg=True). Step-1 diag pull (#2984) CONFIRMED prong 2: trade #2491 is setup_type=intent_reduce, notes.intent_reduce=true, direction=long (buy-to-reduce on held short), shares pkg-8596863669584ed5 with primary short #2490. 27 new unit tests + full PnL-attribution regression green; CI green. VM auto git-sync deployed 1cb6b00 + restarted ict-trader-live (confirmed active on new code). REMAINING: (a) forward verification pending the next real close — at deploy time no trade had closed since 2026-06-07T22:01 (only rejections), so the reconciler path is unexercised on live data yet; (b) demo-row remediation backfill (#2990 backfill-monitor-closed-pnl) recovered 0/16 — BLOCKED by a pre-existing credential-reachability gap in the backfill system-action subprocess (load_runtime_secrets/_lib.sh sources REPO_DIR/.env; account_closed_pnl_for_trade returned None for ALL 16 incl. the 5 demo rows because get_closed_pnl fails auth before the wide fallback can run — the live trader itself has working creds, so this is backfill-subprocess-only). NOT a regression in the fix; logged as new item BL-20260608-CRED below; (c) prong-2 historical backfill of #2491 still needs a 1-line change to backfill_monitor_closed_pnl.py to pass reduce_leg (operator deferred this). Status kept open until forward verification on a live close confirms non-NULL pnl.

items.source: Noticed while investigating the MES (mes_trend_long_1d) IBKR-paper pipeline (PR #2626): order_packages + signal-audit pulls show htf_pullback_trend_2h producing MES order packages even though its config declares symbols:[BTCUSDT] and it routes only to bybit_1.
items.title: Per-strategy `symbols:` does not gate EVALUATION — the multi-symbol tick loop runs every enabled builder on the union of all account symbols, producing cross-product packages no account routes (ORPHANED)
items.description: The tick loop (src/main.py::_resolve_tick_symbols) unions every configured account's symbols ({BTCUSDT, MES}) and evaluates each enabled strategy's signal builder on every symbol in the union, regardless of the strategy's own `symbols:` field in config/strategies.yaml. Observed 2026-06-02: htf_pullback_trend_2h (symbols:[BTCUSDT], routed to bybit_1 only) emitted MES order packages (status 'orphaned'); conversely mes_trend_long_1d (symbols:[MES]) is evaluated on BTCUSDT (the signal audit shows symbol=BTCUSDT eval rows for it, reason 'short_suppressed_long_only'). These cross-product packages are dropped/orphaned because no account routes that (strategy x symbol) combo — the symbol->exchange dispatch gate (coordinator._eligible_for_dispatch) + the account.strategies filter correctly prevent execution, so this is HARMLESS to trading (no wrong-symbol order can fire). But it is wasted per-tick evaluation + journal noise: the orphaned cross-product rows pollute per-strategy orphan-rate stats (cf. BL-20260601-001, whose triage_note already saw these htf_pullback_trend_2h-on-MES orphans). Fix: gate EVALUATION by the strategy's own declared `symbols:` (intersected with its routed accounts' symbols) so a BTCUSDT-only strategy is never run on MES and vice-versa.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or any session touching src/main.py::_resolve_tick_symbols or the per-tick strategy-evaluation loop. Re-pull order_packages and confirm orphaned cross-product packages (htf_pullback_trend_2h on MES, mes_trend_long_1d on BTCUSDT) still appear.
items.resolution_criteria: Strategy signal builders are evaluated only on the symbols the strategy declares in config/strategies.yaml (intersected with account routing), so no cross-product (strategy x non-declared-symbol) order packages are produced. Verify htf_pullback_trend_2h no longer emits MES packages and mes_trend_long_1d no longer emits BTCUSDT packages, with no loss of legitimate signals.
items.resolution_note: Fixed by the per-strategy symbol-scope gate (PR #2643, merged + deployed 2026-06-02, HEAD 3d35e24). intent_multiplexer._collect_intents now skips a strategy whose declared config/strategies.yaml symbols: do not include the current tick symbol (permissive on no-symbols/unknown/config-error), so a strategy is evaluated/emits ONLY on its declared instrument(s) — no cross-product (strategy x non-declared-symbol) packages are produced. Gated at intent EMISSION (not dispatch) so a higher-priority off-symbol strategy can't win a symbol's per-symbol aggregation and starve the legitimate owner. Verified live post-deploy via audit_query: mgc_pullback_1d evaluates MGC-only (zero MES/MHG/BTCUSDT rows) where pre-deploy it ran on all four. Crypto strategies (symbols:[BTCUSDT]) no longer evaluate on MES. Tests: tests/test_intent_symbol_scope.py (4) + 324 intent/coordinator/pipeline regression green. NOTE: the gate lives in the production intent-multiplexer path (STRATEGY=multiplexed_intents); the legacy first-wins pipeline path is not gated but is not the production path.

items.source: S-MLOPT-S8 surfaced 'MES has 0 real closed trades' as the reason cross-symbol transfer is unmeasurable. The 2026-06-03 follow-up traced this to the strategy being correctly inert (no Donchian-30 breakout fired in the ~30h since execution: live promotion). No bug. Filed here so a future /health-review doesn't re-raise 'MES never trades' as a regression.
items.title: Add an alert for 'mes_trend_long_1d_eval rows stop appearing' (catches MES going actually dark, vs side=none being normal)
items.description: MES currently produces a per-tick mes_trend_long_1d_eval row with side=none and reason='no breakout on the latest bar' — that's the strategy doing its job (Donchian-30 long-only on the daily, waiting for a breakout above the prior-30d high; today 7607 vs prior-high 7632.75). The pipeline is wired correctly. But there's currently NO alert that distinguishes 'strategy alive but no setup' (normal) from 'strategy stopped firing eval rows entirely' (real regression — broker dark, candle fetch dead, strategy crashed). Proposal: add a coarse monitor — if signal_audit.jsonl has no `event=mes_trend_long_1d_eval` row in the last 24h, alert. Same for mgc_pullback_1d_eval + mhg_pullback_1d_eval. Doesn't fire on the normal 'waiting for breakout' state; does fire if the strategy stops being evaluated. Composes with the existing liveness watchdog (process-level) — this is strategy-level coverage. Implementation likely belongs near scripts/check_heartbeat.py or as a new check in the health-snapshot timer.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or when the operator notices 'MES never trades' again and we want a faster real-vs-normal discrimination.
items.resolution_criteria: A monitor exists that alerts if any of {mes_trend_long_1d_eval, mgc_pullback_1d_eval, mhg_pullback_1d_eval} hasn't logged an eval row in 24h. False-positive rate verified ≈0 on a normal day (where side=none rows are continuously emitted).

items.description: The tradovate_demo_1 'Hookup checklist (operator)' comment block (config/accounts.yaml ~L414-441, added in the 2026-06-02 Tradovate session) violates the current autonomy canon in three steps: (Step 2) 'Add the seven required env vars to GitHub Actions secrets AND to the live-trader systemd EnvironmentFile (the systemd unit picks them up on restart)' — the operator originates the secret VALUES (Actions secrets) but PROPAGATION to the VM .env/EnvironmentFile is NOT operator work; it's .github/workflows/sync-vm-secrets.yml's job (the canonical broker-credential propagation path, which already declares TRADOVATE_* in its secret set). Hand-editing the systemd EnvironmentFile is the bright-line 'on the VM, edit' pattern credentials-and-vm-mutations forbids. (Steps 3-4) 'Run `python -m src.units.accounts.tradovate.cli.list_accounts` on the VM' + 'Run the smoke test on the VM' — running a CLI on the live VM is runner-dispatchable (a system-action or a diag/trainer-style relay), not operator-attributed manual SSH work. Net: a fresh Claude reading this checklist would anchor on operator-does-VM-work precedent — exactly the failure mode the new skills prevent. HARMLESS to trading (the account is mode:dry_run, dry-run-guard:allow, inert phase-1). config/accounts.yaml is Tier-3 (operator-only) so /health-review cannot edit it.

items.source: Rina performance-review 2026-06-04 — the perf-review skill's documented pulls (/api/bot/trades/closed, /api/bot/order-packages, /api/bot/performance, /api/bot/insights/*) are unreachable from a relay-only session.
items.title: vm-diag-snapshot relay hardcodes /api/diag/ — perf-review's /api/bot/{performance,trades/closed,insights/*} pulls are unreachable from a Trusted-network (relay-only) session
items.description: .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml strips a leading /api/diag/ (line ~134) and curls a hardcoded 'http://127.0.0.1:8001/api/diag/<path>' (line ~185), so the relay can ONLY hit /api/diag/* endpoints. The performance-review SKILL.md § 'Fetching trade data' documents pulling /api/bot/trades/closed?since=&limit=500, /api/bot/order-packages?since=, /api/bot/performance?window=, and /api/bot/insights/{summary,recent,strategy,health} — NONE of which the relay can reach. A relay-only (default Trusted-network) review session therefore cannot get the clean windowed aggregates (/api/bot/performance) or the closed-trade list (/api/bot/trades/closed); it must fall back to /api/diag/journal?table=trades (reject-dominated, 55KB-truncated ~46 rows) + /api/diag/journal?table=insights_history (the M13 'recent' card, which DOES surface the last-20 closed trades — the practical workaround this review used). Net: per-strategy realized-PnL aggregation is hampered relay-only. Fix options: (a) extend vm-diag-snapshot.yml to allow an /api/bot/ prefix passthrough for the read-only GET aggregate endpoints (performance, trades/closed, order-packages); or (b) add /api/diag/performance + /api/diag/trades_closed mirrors; or (c) document insights_history as the canonical relay-only closed-trade source in the perf-review skill. Mirrors the BL-20260529-004 insights-relay-reach fix (which added insights_history/insights_usage to _JOURNAL_TABLES).
items.trigger_condition: Any /performance-review or /health-review run from a relay-only session that needs /api/bot/ aggregate data; or next time someone extends the diag relay.
items.resolution_criteria: Either the vm-diag relay can return /api/bot/performance + /api/bot/trades/closed (passthrough or /api/diag mirror), verified by a relay pull; OR the perf-review SKILL.md is updated to use the relay-reachable substitutes (journal?table=trades + insights_history) as the canonical relay-only path so a future session doesn't assume the /api/bot/ pulls work.
items.resolution_note: FIXED — extended .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml so the relay can reach an ALLOWLISTED set of read-only /api/bot/* Tier-1 GET endpoints (performance, trades/closed, order-packages, insights/*, health/*, shadow/*, etc.) in addition to /api/diag/*. Default upstream stays /api/diag/; an api/bot/* path is routed to /api/bot/ only if its base (query stripped) is in the explicit allowlist, else rejected. curl URL switched to ${diag_upstream_prefix}${diag_path_safe}. Verified the routing logic + YAML parse locally (diag unchanged; allowlisted bot paths route correctly; non-allowlisted bot paths reject). A future /performance-review can now pull /api/bot/performance + /api/bot/trades/closed cleanly relay-only. Pending merge.

items.source: Closing BL-20260529-002 (deploy-restart gate fixed by #2595). Its SECONDARY gap is unaddressed and should not be lost on the close.
items.title: ict-web-api has no VM-side self-heal; the only autonomous recover lever (GitHub vm-web-api-recover) is webhook-flaky exactly when needed
items.description: BL-20260529-002's PRIMARY gap (docs-commit trader bounce) was fixed by PR #2595. Its SECONDARY gap remains: ict-web-api.service has no on-VM auto-restart-on-unhealthy guard, and the only autonomous recovery path (the GitHub-dispatched vm-web-api-recover workflow) is subject to the issues.opened webhook batch-drop (BL-20260527-001) precisely during a deploy bounce when the read surface is down — observed 2026-05-29 (#2297/#2299/#2300 all dropped) and the #2373 recover that ran mid-bounce failed. With the PRIMARY gap closed the read-surface blind windows are now rarer (docs commits no longer bounce anything), but a genuine web-api crash still has no VM-side self-heal. Fix: add a systemd watchdog/timer (mirror ict-liveness-watchdog / ict-ib-gateway-watchdog) that probes local 127.0.0.1:8001/api/health every ~2 min and restarts ict-web-api.service on N consecutive failures, with restart-cap + cooldown so a genuine crashloop alert-escalates rather than loops. Removes the webhook dependency for read-surface recovery.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or next time a vm-web-api-recover dispatch is webhook-dropped or ict-web-api is observed down with no autonomous recovery.
items.resolution_criteria: ict-web-api gets a VM-side auto-restart-on-unhealthy guard (systemd timer probing local /api/health) that self-heals the read surface without a webhook-dependent GitHub dispatch; verified by inducing/observing one web-api outage that the watchdog recovers autonomously. Tier-2 deploy/runtime change — operator-approved PR.

check_log.note: FIX BUILT (pending merge+deploy+verify). scripts/check_web_api.py — stdlib-only watchdog probing http://127.0.0.1:8001/api/health every 2 min (ict-web-api-watchdog.{service,timer}); after 2 consecutive failures runs scripts/ops/restart_web_api.sh, guard-railed --max-restarts 3 + --cooldown-min 10 (crashloop -> alert-escalate, not loop). Decision state machine is byte-identical to the proven check_ib_gateway.py::decide. --auto-restart enabled in the unit (read-only API = low blast radius). Registered in test_s012 EXPECTED_SERVICES + deployment-ops.md; timer auto-enabled by install_systemd_units.sh. Tests: tests/test_check_web_api.py (9). POST-DEPLOY VERIFY: confirm the timer is active + a healthy probe in journalctl (healthy path); the restart path proves out on a real/induced wedge. Closes the gap where the only web-api recovery lever (GitHub vm-web-api-recover) is webhook-flaky.

NEW EVIDENCE of the gap this item targets: this session, /api/bot/order-packages + /api/bot/config returned HTTP 500 transiently around the ~06:19/07:16 trader restarts (the heavier bot routes erroring while /api/diag/*, the Streamlit routine-poll set, and the insights cache stayed 200). They SELF-RECOVERED (config 200 + 8 Streamlit routes 200 OK by 08:06-08:20, same web-api PID 386690 = no restart) — so no recovery lever was needed THIS time, but it confirms the heavier endpoints wobble around restarts and the only autonomous recover (vm-web-api-recover) is still webhook-flaky. The scripts/check_web_api.py VM-side watchdog this item proposes is still pending merge+deploy. Status unchanged (open).

items.source: The revived ict-health-snapshot writer's first live runs grade `accounts_api` as failing (M13 card 6/7, grade watch) while the trader is healthy + trading — surfaced verifying BL-20260529-005.
items.title: check_accounts_api fails in the standalone health-snapshot writer context (account_balance() returns None without the trader's live exchange clients) — card shows a perpetual accounts_api 'watch'
items.description: src/runtime/health.py::check_accounts_api reuses src.bot.data_loaders.account_balance() per account and WARNs on any None. In the trader PROCESS this works (it holds the live exchange/IB clients). But ict-health-snapshot.service runs write_health_snapshot.py as a SEPARATE oneshot that has no such clients (e.g. the local smoke logged 'account_balance(ib_paper): ib_insync is not installed' and Bybit balance needs a live socket), so account_balance() returns None -> accounts_api WARN -> the M13 health card sits at 6/7 'watch' with accounts_api failing even though the live trader's accounts are fine. This is a CONTEXT artifact of running the check outside the trader, not a real outage (verified: the trader dispatched real trade 2428 + is healthy). Net effect: the card never shows fully-green when it should. Fix options: (a) point check_accounts_api at the CONNECTION-FREE balance snapshot (runtime_logs/balance_snapshots.json — the same source /api/bot/accounts/balances uses, 'never opens an exchange socket'), so it reflects the last recorded balance instead of trying to connect; or (b) have the standalone writer skip/soften the accounts_api check (env flag) when not in-process; or (c) accept the warn as a known standalone limitation and document it. (a) is the cleanest — it makes the check connection-free + context-independent. Tier-1 code (observability); not a money-path change.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever the dashboard health card shows accounts_api failing while the live trader is confirmed healthy.
items.resolution_criteria: check_accounts_api reports ok in the standalone ict-health-snapshot writer context when the trader's accounts are healthy (e.g. by reading the connection-free balance snapshot), so the M13 health card grades `good`/ok on a healthy system instead of a perpetual accounts_api `watch`. Verify via insights_history (endpoint=health) after the fix deploys.

items.source: /health-review 2026-06-06: /api/diag/journalctl relay calls for ict-trader-live.service (lines=90) AND ict-liveness-watchdog.service (lines=70) both failed (run failed / curl --fail/--max-time), while journalctl?unit=ict-web-api.service (lines=120), status, config, journal, and log_file all succeeded.
items.title: Observability gap: /api/diag/journalctl fails for high-volume units (ict-trader-live) under the relay's curl --max-time, blocking restart-cause root-cause exactly when needed
items.description: When investigating the 2026-06-06 trader restart churn (BL-20260605-001), the diag journalctl pulls for ict-trader-live.service and ict-liveness-watchdog.service both returned 'run failed' via the vm-diag relay, while the lighter ict-web-api.service journalctl (and status/config/journal/log_file) succeeded. The trader logs a large volume per tick, so a journalctl tail of 70-120 lines likely exceeds the relay's `curl -sS --fail --max-time 40` budget (slow/large response -> curl exit 28/timeout -> workflow reports failure), OR the response byte size trips the same ~414KB cap noted elsewhere. Net effect: the single most useful pull for diagnosing a restart loop (the trader + watchdog journals) is the one that fails, so the autoheal-vs-crash-vs-IB-wedge trigger could not be captured this session. Candidate fixes (Tier-1/2): (a) raise the journalctl curl --max-time in vm-diag-snapshot.yml for journalctl paths; (b) add `since=`/`until=` narrowing support guidance so callers pull a small time-window instead of N lines; (c) add a dedicated lightweight 'restart-cause' diag (e.g. systemctl show --property=NRestarts,ActiveEnterTimestamp + the last N ExecStart lines) that returns small output; (d) gzip/cap journalctl output server-side. Until fixed, restart-cause investigation must use systemctl show timestamps + smaller line counts.

items.source: Session 2026-06-07: built the cancel-queued-runs workflow (#2935/#2938) to clear 23 stale 'queued' Actions runs dated 2026-05-15. The runner (with actions:write) found all 23 but cancelled 0 — both `gh run cancel` and the force-cancel endpoint return HTTP 500 for every one.
items.title: 23 zombie 'queued' Actions runs (2026-05-15) are un-cancellable — GitHub returns HTTP 500 on both cancel and force-cancel
items.description: 23 workflow runs have been stuck in 'queued' since 2026-05-15 (ids 25906694928..25907269254). They never got a runner and consume no compute, but clutter the Actions list. The cancel-queued-runs workflow added this session (runner-path, since the PM-side MCP token lacks actions:write) lists them correctly and attempts normal cancel then force-cancel (POST repos/.../actions/runs/{id}/force-cancel) — both return `HTTP 500` for all 23, so the workflow cancels 0. A 500 (not 403/422) means it is a GitHub server-side failure on these specific orphaned runs; nothing a runner or the Actions UI (same endpoints) can do. The cancel-queued-runs workflow itself is sound and reusable for any FUTURE cancellable queued backlog — these particular runs are the exception. Resolution is GitHub-side: GitHub may garbage-collect them eventually, or GitHub Support can purge zombie runs. No code change in this repo will clear them. Note runs can't be DELETED either (the delete-run endpoint only accepts completed runs, not queued). Tracked here only so a future health-review doesn't re-investigate the same inert clutter.
items.trigger_condition: A future health-review or session notices the 23 stale queued runs and considers re-attempting cancellation, or wonders whether cancel-queued-runs is broken.
items.resolution_criteria: The 23 stale 2026-05-15 queued runs no longer appear in the Actions list (GitHub-side GC or Support purge). No repo change is expected to satisfy this; close when the runs are gone or the operator decides to leave them.

items.title: PR #3007 CI never triggered after push (0 check runs in 7+ h on SHA e286c4ea)
items.description: After opening PR #3007 against main from claude/vibrant-babbage-9Wbls, mcp__github__pull_request_read method=get_status returned state='pending', total_count=0 across 4+ polls over a 7-hour window. items.source: Self-inflicted incident: vm-web-api-recover run 27150313822 (issue #3026) restarted a healthy ict-web-api.service, then the wrapper's 15-second post-restart health probe failed because cold-start exceeded 15 s, leaving the unit in unknown state and emitting a misleading 'unit did not return to a serving state' error.
items.title: vm-web-api-recover wrapper's 15 s post-restart health probe is too tight for cold-start of ict-web-api.service
items.description: scripts/ops/restart_web_api.sh probes /api/health for ~15 s after the systemctl restart returns. Observed timeline 16:02:23–16:03:01 UTC: systemd stopped uvicorn cleanly (16:02:23–16:02:41), check_data_dir.sh ran (16:02:43–16:02:44), systemd reported 'Started ICT Trading Bot — dashboard API' at 16:02:44, but uvicorn didn't bind port 8001 within the wrapper's 15 s probe window (curl exit 7 at 16:03:01). At 16:13:49 /api/diag/status returned 200 OK — so uvicorn DID come up, just slowly. The 15 s probe ceiling needs widening (suggest 60 s) or the wrapper should poll until ExecStartPost completes systemd-side. Secondary issue: the wrapper's failure message implies the unit is wedged, which led this session to escalate to the operator unnecessarily. Both behaviours wasted operator attention. Lower priority than BL-20260609-001's CI trigger bug but worth a small PR.
